fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 13-year-old female brought by school counselor after expressing wish to 'disappear' due to ongoing conflict with best friend.
fields.Subjective: Patient is a 13-year-old female presenting after disclosing to school counselor that she wishes she could disappear following a major falling-out with her best friend of five years. The friend posted hurtful things about patient on social media and turned their friend group against her. Patient reports passive thoughts over past three days wishing she did not have to go to school or exist in this situation, but denies any plan, method, or intent to harm herself. She clarifies she just wants the social pain to stop. Sleep disrupted, appetite decreased. Still enjoys reading and spending time with her older sister. Mother is supportive. No prior mental health concerns. (Social Worker Carla Martinez, LCSW)

Mental_Status_Examination.Thought_Content: Passive suicidal ideation without plan or intent; social pain

fields.Diagnostic_Impression: Adjustment disorder with depressed mood related to peer conflict and social loss; passive suicidal ideation.
fields.Psychosocial_Context: Significant friendship loss and peer conflict; cyberbullying component; enjoys reading; close with older sister; mother supportive; no prior mental health concerns.
Assessment_and_Plan: Low risk: passive SI without plan or intent in context of normal adolescent social crisis.
Strong protective factors: mother and sister support, reading interest, good insight.
Normal grief reaction to significant friendship loss.
School intervention for cyberbullying and peer mediation.
Brief counseling for coping with peer conflict and friendship loss.
Help develop new peer connections; explore clubs related to reading interest.
Safety plan with mother and counselor.
Follow-up with school counselor and brief outpatient therapy.
Safe for discharge with appropriate support.
fields.Author: Carla Martinez, LCSW (Social Worker)
